title: Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (HST) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (HST) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% growth rate is estimated based on the recent free cash flow generation and the fundamental outlook for the REIT - Hotel & Motel sector.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A discount rate of 9.0% reflects the company's cost of capital, weighing systemic risks and consistent cash generation.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 2.0% is selected as the terminal growth rate, aligning with long-term macroeconomic GDP expectations.

thesisParagraphs: Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. currently generates stable free cash flows, which are critical for its valuation in the REIT - Hotel & Motel space. At a current price of $19.1, the market has embedded specific growth expectations into the stock.
Using a discounted cash flow model, we estimate an intrinsic value of $26.99. The current valuation suggests a margin of safety of 41.29%, leading to a UNDERVALUED verdict.
verdictFooter: Based on actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why was a 3.0% growth rate used?
faq.answer: The rate aligns with normalized historical and projected fundamental growth trends in the REIT - Hotel & Motel sector.

faq.question: What does the discount rate reflect?
faq.answer: The WACC represents the blended cost of equity and debt, capturing the risk profile of the cash flows.

faq.question: Is this financial advice?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is an AI-generated model based on quantitative inputs and rigid formulas.
